## Results

This family estimates an observational session-dose association on the logit
scale, per 1 SD of period session count. Positive slopes mean larger conditional
gains are associated with higher dose in that period.

In plain terms: the slope is the change in outcome per additional session (per
one extra dose unit) — how much more children tend to gain when they get more
sessions in a period.

```{python}
# | echo: false
dose_slope_summary = _csv("dose_slope_summary.csv")
dose_slope_summary if dose_slope_summary is not None else "No dose-slope summary."
```

_How to read the dose-slope table:_ each row is one estimated quantity. `mean`
is the posterior mean slope in logit units per 1 SD of sessions (bigger positive
= more gain per extra dose); `lo` and `hi` are the ends of the 89% credible
interval (the plausible range); `p_pos` is the probability the slope is above
zero (near 1 favours benefit, near 0.5 is no clear direction). The
`sigma_dose_between_period` row, when present, is how much the slope varies from
period to period — small means periods share a common dose association.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if dose_slope_summary is not None and len(dose_slope_summary):
    _main = dose_slope_summary.iloc[0]
    print(
        f"**Headline dose association (`{_main.term}`):** posterior mean "
        f"{_main['mean']:+.3f} logit units per 1 SD sessions "
        f"(50% CrI {_main.lo50:+.3f} to {_main.hi50:+.3f}; "
        f"89% {_main.lo:+.3f} to {_main.hi:+.3f}); "
        f"P(> 0) = {_main.p_pos:.3f}."
    )
    if "sigma_dose_between_period" in set(dose_slope_summary.term):
        _s = dose_slope_summary[
            dose_slope_summary.term == "sigma_dose_between_period"
        ].iloc[0]
        print(
            f"\n\nBetween-period slope SD: mean {_s['mean']:.3f} "
            f"(50% CrI {_s.lo50:.3f} to {_s.hi50:.3f}; "
            f"89% {_s.lo:.3f} to {_s.hi:.3f}). Small values mean the "
            "period slopes shrink toward a common dose association."
        )
```

_Observational dose-response._ Dose is not randomised and may be confounded by latent general ability and
availability. Compare the primary fit with its ability-adjusted or pooled-dose
companion before treating the slope as robust.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# Optional comparison outputs, shown when copied beside this report.
_comp = _csv("dose_loo_compare.csv")
_comp if _comp is not None else _csv("did_dose_loo_compare.csv")
```

_How to read the comparison table:_ this ranks the dose model against its
companion by out-of-sample fit (leave-one-out cross-validation). The top row is
the best-predicting model; `elpd_diff` is how far each other model falls behind
it (0 for the top row, more negative = worse), and `se_diff` is the uncertainty
in that gap — a difference only counts as clear when it is several times its own
`se_diff`.
